phases.result:
Planning pipeline complete. Story persisted and marked `agent::ready`.

## Summary

**Story #7 — "Split client name into firstName and lastName"** — [issue link](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-24-brownfield-longitudinal-mandrel-2c64f615/issues/7) · `agent::ready` · cohort `plan-run::daa252a1`

Authored as a **single cohesive Story** (default-single policy): schema, migration, service, repository, and read-surface changes all move together as one capability slice.

### Key findings that shaped the plan
- **Clients = customers.** `/clients` is the canonical route prefix backed by the same service/repo/table as the deprecated `/customers` alias — so this change lives in the customer stack.
- **Split rule as a single source of truth.** The "before-last-space / last-word / single-word→last-name" rule must be applied identically on the legacy-`name` write path and the startup backfill (one shared helper).
- **Legacy `name` writes preserved.** Existing validation tests still POST `name` and expect 422s on empty/non-string/unknown fields — so the schema must accept `firstName`/`lastName` **and** legacy `name` while still rejecting unknowns.
- **"Current" name is load-bearing.** The receivables report and order responses currently surface `orders.customer_name` — a snapshot frozen at order creation. The request's "current full name" wording means these must derive from the live client record, so I scoped both surfaces into the current-name invariant.
- **Startup migration.** Migrations run at boot via `openDb() → runMigrations`, so a new SQL migration adds the columns and backfills in place, staying idempotent/transactional.

### Gates
- Gate #1 (intent) and Gate #2 (pre-persist) auto-proceeded under `--yes`.
- Critics: consolidation + pre-mortem both skipped (dispatch:false, N=1); one advisory text-hygiene finding (dangling `§` citations) folded in by anchoring to `docs/CONVENTIONS.md`.
- Persist ran dry-run first (all hard gates green), then live. Only a soft advisory remained — Spec at ~260 words vs the 250 soft budget — non-gating.

Per the benchmark constraints, I ran **only** the planning pipeline. Deliver when ready with `/deliver 7`.

Story #7 delivered — terminal status **`landed`**.

## Delivery complete

`/deliver 7 --yes` ran the single-Story engine inline end-to-end:

- **Discovery** — the change request maps to Story **#7** *"Split client name into firstName and lastName"* (`agent::ready`). The v2 model is Story-only, so there was no separate Epic to resolve — #7 *is* the plan, delivered as-is (no re-planning).
- **Implementation** on `story-7` (worktree-isolated):
  - `src/lib/name.js` — single shared `splitName`/`fullName` rule (everything before the last space → firstName, last word → lastName, single-word → empty firstName).
  - `006-client-names.sql` — adds `first_name`/`last_name`, backfills existing rows in place via the same rule (SQL split validated to match the JS helper), drops `name`; idempotent via `runMigrations`.
  - Customer writes accept `firstName`/`lastName` and still accept legacy `name` (split); responses expose both parts, no standalone `name`.
  - Order reads + receivables derive the **current** full name from the live customer row (repository JOIN), so renames are reflected rather than frozen at order creation.
- **Verification** — full suite **149 pass / 0 fail**; `lint` and `typecheck` exit 0.
- **Acceptance self-eval** — inline verdict scored **proceed** (8/8 criteria `met`).
- **Close & land** — gates passed, code review 0 findings, PR **#8** opened and **merged** to `main`, Story flipped to `agent::done`.

Terminal envelope: `status: landed`, phase `post-land`, PR #8 `MERGED`, checks `success`. The one degraded tail item (`baseFastForward: skipped — dirty-tree`) is due to pre-existing unrelated dirt in the main checkout from another session; the merge stands and this does not affect the delivery.
